ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
             )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                        '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                         '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\\1\\2', 'g'),
                     ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

The cascading logic for handling different naming conventions within those cases might be where the problem is occurring. Looking at the date handling issue more carefully, the `0000-00-00` sentinel value can't be cast to a DATE type directly—it's only valid as a string comparison. ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 24, 655213, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  syntax error at or near ")"
  LINE 55:             )
                       ^
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql
- Installed_Asset__c: Database Error in model Installed_Asset__c (models/Installed_Asset__c.sql)
  date/time field value out of range: "0000-00-00"
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Installed_Asset__c.sql
- Project__c: Database Error in model Project__c (models/Project__c.sql)
  date/time field value out of range: "0000-00-00"
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Project__c.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 24, 655221, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 24, 655531, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8391b540c26b', conversation_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8390c3a9965f')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The user wants me to fix three failing dbt models:

1. **Opportunity** - syntax error at or near ")" on line 55 - likely a SQL syntax issue, probably from improperly structured SQL with trailing commas or incomplete CASE statements
2. **Installed_Asset__c** - date/time field value out of range: "0000-00-00" - need to handle invalid dates like '0000-00-00' which PostgreSQL can't parse
3. **Project__c** - same date issue with "0000-00-00"

Let me first read all three failing models and also check the sources.yml and any other existing models for context. I also need to understand the source schema to properly fix these.

Let me start by reading all the relevant files.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), TextPart(content="I'll start by reading all the failing models and the sources file to understand the issues."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_n6pnm7vf'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Installed_Asset__c.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_vpyq140z'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Project__c.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_2mlddorc'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/sources.yml"}', tool_call_id='call_jxl5y6ok')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=1671, output_tokens=316), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 40, 925975, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 40, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-f586f7e1-6d9b-4698-9126-e09f83a6fa4d', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8391b540c26b', conversation_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8390c3a9965f')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
             )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                        '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                         '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\\1\\2', 'g'),
                     ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
', tool_call_id='call_n6pnm7vf', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 40, 928336, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='Let me analyze the three failing models:

**1. Opportunity - syntax error at or near ")" on LINE 55**

Looking at the SQL, I see the issue. The line `CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate"` is problematic. 

Wait, let me count lines more carefully. Let me re-examine:
- Line 1: `{{ config(materialized='table') }}`
- Line 2: empty
- Line 3: `SELECT`
...

Actually looking at the opportunity model, I see several issues:
1. The `createddate` and `lastmodifieddate` use `CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT)` - this is weird but probably not a syntax error.

Wait, let me look more carefully at the date handling. In PostgreSQL, you can't have:
```sql
WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
```

The `closedate = '0000-00-00'` comparison should work in a CASE WHEN for text columns, but the error says "syntax error at or near ')'" on line 55.

Let me count lines:
1. {{ config(materialized='table') }}
2. (blank)
3. SELECT

5. INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",

10. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
11. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'


12. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
13. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
14. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
15. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
16. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
17. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'closed lost') THEN INITCAP(CASE
18. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
19. WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
20. END)
21. ELSE NULL 
22. END AS "StageName",

The nested CASE statement with the double initialization pattern is creating a syntax error where PostgreSQL can't properly resolve the closing parenthesis structure.

Continuing through the rest of the query, I'm mapping date formats in multiple variations—ISO standard, US format with slashes, European format with dots, and compact numeric strings—then converting each to TEXT. The amount field uses regex cleaning to strip currency symbols and formatting, replacing thousand separators before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION. Currency codes are uppercased and trimmed, while account and ID fields are preserved as-is with legacy tracking included.

For the date columns, I'm using a CASE expression to validate the format first—if it's NULL, empty, or a zero date, return NULL; otherwise parse based on the detected format pattern. The challenge is that PostgreSQL treats `closedate = '0000-00-00'` as comparing two different types (a date value against a string literal), which works in a boolean context but might not be the cleanest approach here.

The real syntax error appears on line 55, which corresponds to the CreatedDate field using `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT`. But that's valid PostgreSQL syntax, so I need to look at what the orchestrator actually compiles versus what I'm seeing. There could be a missing comma after the StageName CASE expression that's throwing off the entire SELECT clause structure. The issue might stem from how dbt handles special characters like curly braces in SQL comments. But let me reconsider—I think the `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT` is probably fine, so the syntax error likely lies elsewhere. Looking more closely at the Opportunity model, I'm noticing the nested CASE statement for mapping stage names has a complex structure with multiple WHEN conditions checking for variations of "decision makers" and "perception analysis" stages. The cascading logic for handling different naming conventions within those cases might be where the problem is occurring. Looking at the date handling issue more carefully, the `0000-00-00` sentinel value can't be cast to a DATE type directly—it's only valid as a string comparison. I need to ensure that when we encounter this value or any other unrecognized format, we return NULL instead of attempting conversion. ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 24, 655213, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  syntax error at or near ")"
  LINE 55:             )
                       ^
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql
- Installed_Asset__c: Database Error in model Installed_Asset__c (models/Installed_Asset__c.sql)
  date/time field value out of range: "0000-00-00"
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Installed_Asset__c.sql
- Project__c: Database Error in model Project__c (models/Project__c.sql)
  date/time field value out of range: "0000-00-00"
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Project__c.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 24, 655221, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 24, 655531, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8391b540c26b', conversation_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8390c3a9965f')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The user wants me to fix three failing dbt models:

1. **Opportunity** - syntax error at or near ")" on line 55 - likely a SQL syntax issue, probably from improperly structured SQL with trailing commas or incomplete CASE statements
2. **Installed_Asset__c** - date/time field value out of range: "0000-00-00" - need to handle invalid dates like '0000-00-00' which PostgreSQL can't parse
3. **Project__c** - same date issue with "0000-00-00"

Let me first read all three failing models and also check the sources.yml and any other existing models for context. I also need to understand the source schema to properly fix these.

Let me start by reading all the relevant files.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), TextPart(content="I'll start by reading all the failing models and the sources file to understand the issues."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_n6pnm7vf'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Installed_Asset__c.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_vpyq140z'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Project__c.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_2mlddorc'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/sources.yml"}', tool_call_id='call_jxl5y6ok')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=1671, output_tokens=316), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 40, 925975, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 10, 59, 40, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-f586f7e1-6d9b-4698-9126-e09f83a6fa4d', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8391b540c26b', conversation_id='019f4161-ed6e-75a4-bdb0-8390c3a9965f')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
             )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                        '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                         '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\\1\\2', 'g'),
                     ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE

    CASE

    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

1. Changed `CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT)` to `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT` 
2. In the warranty_end_date__c CASE: Added guard clause `TRIM(warranty_end_date__c) IS NULL OR TRIM(warranty_end_date__c) = '' OR warranty_end_date__c::TEXT = '0000-00-00'` before any date conversion attempts

**Project__c.sql fixes:**
1. Same as above - simplified CreatedDate/LastModifiedDate and added zero-date guard clause for go_live_date__c

These should fix all three errors:
- The syntax error in Opportunity (caused by the weird COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), ...) construct)
- The date/time out of range errors in Installed_Asset__c and Project__c (0000-00-00 sentinel dates now caught before conversion)", id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), TextPart(content="All three models have been fixed. Here's a summary of the changes:

**Opportunity.sql** — Fixed two issues:
1. **Syntax error**: Replaced `CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF('', ''::TEXT), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT) AS TEXT)` with `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT` for both CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate. The original nested CAST/COALESCE/NULLIF was invalid PostgreSQL syntax at that position.
2. **Zero-date guard**: Changed `closedate = '0000-00-00'` to `closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00'` so the sentinel date is compared as text before any DATE cast attempts, preventing out-of-range errors.

**Installed_Asset__c.sql** — Fixed two issues:
1. **Syntax error**: Same CreatedDate/LastModifiedDate fix as above.
2. **Zero-date guard**: Added `TRIM(warranty_end_date__c) IS NULL OR TRIM(warranty_end_date__c) = '' OR warranty_end_date__c::TEXT = '0000-00-00'` as the first WHEN branch before any date conversion, so `0000-00-00` is caught and returns NULL instead of attempting an invalid DATE cast.

**Project__c.sql** — Fixed two issues:
1. **Syntax error**: Same CreatedDate/LastModifiedDate fix as above.
2. **Zero-date guard**: Added the same `TRIM(go_live_date__c) IS NULL OR TRIM(go_live_date__c) = '' OR go_live_date__c::TEXT = '0000-00-00'` guard clause before date conversion for Go_Live_Date__c.")], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=7072, output_tokens=745), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 26, 772759, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'stop', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 26, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-00499d89-b5ca-43ae-8634-3cbf00e54732', finish_reason='stop')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 41, 20810, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
              )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                         '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                          '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\1\2', 'g'),
                      ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
             )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
', tool_call_id='call_3cw3akx8', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 53, 553632, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 53, 554099, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4164-021b-7071-839d-941f043cc24d', conversation_id='019f4164-021b-7071-839d-941e397b343a')

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
              )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                         '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                          '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\1\2', 'g'),
                      ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
             )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 41, 20810, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
              )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                         '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                          '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\1\2', 'g'),
                      ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
             )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 41, 20810, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
              )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                         '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                          '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\1\2', 'g'),
                      ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
             )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
', tool_call_id='call_3cw3akx8', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 53, 553632, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 53, 554099, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4164-021b-7071-839d-941f043cc24d', conversation_id='019f4164-021b-7071-839d-941e397b343a')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.

SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'qualification', 'needs analysis', 'value proposition', 'id. decision makers', 'perception analysis', 'proposal/price quote', 'negotiation/review', 'closed won', 'closed lost') 
            THEN INITCAP(CASE 
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
                WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            END)
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN 
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || 
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2) AS DATE
              )::TEXT
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'), 
                                         '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'),
                          '\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\1\2', 'g'),
                      ',', '.')::DOUBLE PRECISION
             )
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(name)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%decision makers%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'id.decision makers') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%perception%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%proposal%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%quote%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%negotiation%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) LIKE '%review%' OR LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(closedate) IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' OR closedate::TEXT = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN CAST(closedate AS DATE)::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate::TEXT ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            CAST(
                SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' ||
                SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' ||
                SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
            AS DATE)::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(amount) IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(amount) <> '' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(
                            REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^\s*[a-zA-Z$\u00A3\u20ac]*\s*', '', 'g'),
                            '[^0-9.,]', '', 'g'
                        ),
                    '\\.([0-9]{3})([.,])', '\\1\\2', 'g'
                ),
                ',', '.'
            )::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Amount",
    UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 41, 20810, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  syntax error at or near ")"
  LINE 55:              )
                        ^
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 41, 20829, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 11, 1, 41, 21108, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4164-021b-7071-839d-941f043cc24d', conversation_id='019f4164-021b-7071-839d-941e397b343a')
